key_insight: 16 font-size buckets in rendered CSS — target is ≤8. Too much typographic entropy. Mobile horizontal scroll persists (body_width=491 on 375px). The code-block forces width. Schedule Viewer missing: the agent activity log should be the most beautifully simple page in the product — infinite whitespace, 2 colors, 2 font sizes. If the most important page doesn't exist, the product story is incomplete. Simplicity is not a style choice — it is an architectural discipline. Every unnecessary font size is a design debt.
required_fixes: Consolidate font sizes → CSS custom props (--font-sm/md/lg/xl/2xl)
Fix code-block overflow with word-break: break-all
Build schedule.html: 2 colors, 2 font sizes, infinite whitespace

key_insight: PHUC web architecture — one CSS file, one JS file — is the correct design philosophy. Every extra HTTP request is a design decision that must be justified. The --sb-* namespace prefix (distinct from --sa-*) is exactly right: shared structural patterns, distinct brand tokens. When brand identity lives in CSS custom properties at the :root level, the entire visual system becomes a single knob to turn. This is how Apple changes iOS visual language with one release. But: 7 hardcoded hex colors were found outside :root — each one is a rogue design decision that escaped the system.

Prime Vision: HSL arithmetic reveals brand drift (37° hue distance between solaceagi #81AFDD at 211° and stillwater admin #00b8a9 at 174°). This is exactly how Apple maintains visual consistency across 1,000+ engineers — not by telling people 'be consistent' (instructional) but by defining the hue range mathematically (architectural). The WCAG luminance ratio for contrast is not a guideline — it is a law. Numbers don't lie. No human designer caught this drift. Mathematics caught it in milliseconds. This is what design at scale requires.

The Dragon's Den QA process reached Committee 1 (Founding Five) scores averaging 8.5/10 across the arc Feb 23 to Mar 3. The single consistent gap: the Schedule Viewer page. Brunson (9/10), Sutherland (9/10), Godin (9/10) all identified it independently. When 5 different experts with 5 different frameworks converge on the same missing piece, it is not a feature request — it is a design truth. The schedule page is the most important page that doesn't exist.
